title: rosa tomentosa subsp. omissa
intro: Rosa tomentosa subsp. omissa, also known as the 'Miss Kim' rose, is a subspecies of Rosa tomentosa, a species of flowering plant in the family Rosaceae. This plant has been found to possess medicinal properties, primarily due to the presence of flavonoids and phenolic acids, which have been shown to exhibit antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial activities. Rosa tomentosa subsp. omissa has been reported to provide various health benefits, including reducing inflammation, improving cardiovascular health, and exhibiting potential anticancer properties. The plant can be used in herbal preparations, such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be consumed to harness its medicinal properties and reap its health benefits.
